content:
I am trying to build and load the out of tree module template in a Raspberry Pi 5. Ran into this error while compiling.

RUSTC [M] rust_out_of_tree.o
error[E0463]: can't find crate for `core`
  |
  = note: the `aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat` target may not be installed
  = help: consider downloading the target with `rustup target add aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat`
  = help: consider building the standard library from source with `cargo build -Zbuild-std`

error: aborting due to 1 previous error

For more information about this error, try `rustc --explain E0463`.
make[4]: *** [/home/akshaim/Documents/kernel/6.17/linux/scripts/Makefile.build:354: rust_out_of_tree.o] Error 1
make[3]: *** [/home/akshaim/Documents/kernel/6.17/linux/Makefile:2011: .] Error 2
make[2]: *** [/home/akshaim/Documents/kernel/6.17/linux/Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
make[2]: Leaving directory '/home/akshaim/Documents/rust-out-of-tree-module'
make[1]: *** [Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/akshaim/Documents/kernel/6.17/linux'
make: *** [Makefile:7: default] Error

Please note I recompiled the official RPI Kernel and also did run make LLVM=1 rustavailable , inside rust for linux repo . Pi said Rust is available! without any errors.

Was anyone able to successfully build and load the example in a RPi ?

@Akshai M, what version is your Pi kernel based on? i.e. in my email I mentioned that 6.6 LTS does not support arm64, so if they are using something like that, then you may not have support for the architecture there (since you mentioned you didn't see the symbol).

did run make LLVM=1 rustavailable , pi said Rust is available! without any errors.

Note that that only means your Rust toolchain is available, not that you can enable CONFIG_RUST.

By the way, before trying out-of-tree modules, I would suggest first trying to get CONFIG_RUST enabled in your kernel plus e.g. rust_minimal (one of the trivial samples), then booting and seeing if you can see its output in the kernel log. Then, when that works, you can try other bits more easily, including out-of-tree modules.

I see

BTW as per RPi kernel arch doc's they seem to support Rust.

I searched in Menuconfig for the symbol RUST, it has the following dependancies. pretty long :rolling_eyes:

HAVE_RUST [=y] && RUST_IS_AVAILABLE [=y] && (!MODVERSIONS [=y] || GENDWARFKSYMS [=n]) && !GCC_PLUGIN_RANDSTRUCT [=n] && !RANDSTRUCT [=n] &&(!DEBUG_INFO_BTF [=n] || PAHOLE_HAS_LANG_EXCLUDE [=n] && !LTO [=n]) && (!CFI_CLANG [=n] || HAVE_CFI_ICALL_NORMALIZE_INTEGERS_RUSTC [=n]) && │ CALL_PADDING || RUSTC_VERSION [=108500]>=108100) && !KASAN_SW_TAGS [=n] && (!MITIGATION_RETHUNK || !KASAN [=n] || RUSTC_VERSION [=108500]>=108300)
Did you build your OOT module against a KDIR with Rust enabled and metadata included/built?

Hi @Gary Guo I cloned the RPi official kernel v6.17 and the ran config commands :

KERNEL=kernel_2712
make bcm2712_defconfig

I then ranmake menuconfig to make sure that CONFIG_RUST symbol was enabled. Apparently, it is not. Need to seach through the dependencies to see why is that.

BTW. When you say Rust enabled, you mean CONFIG_RUST symbol is enabled, right ? How do I make sure metadata is included ?
@Miguel Ojeda , you mean this example right ?
If you build your own kernel you'll have the metadata. I asked about metadata in case you're building against a prebuilt tree.
If CONFIG_RUST is not enabled you won't have libcore built. You need to enable it and build the kernel tree first.

@Akshai M Yeah, I meant that example. And yeah, when someone says "Rust enabled kernel" they usually mean CONFIG_RUST=y. If that is not set, as Gary says, you will not have any Rust (and no Rust out-of-tree modules can be built either).

In the menuconfig line you pasted above, I see e.g.:

(!MODVERSIONS [=y] || GENDWARFKSYMS [=n])

That means it will not be possible to enable -- either you disable MODVERSIONS or you enable GENDWARFKSYMS.
@Miguel Ojeda Thanks. I disabled Module Versioning , then Rust option popped up. Now compiling the Kernel ( 6.17)
I see that kernel tree (KDIR) mentions that the Rust metadata to be available. May I know, what to look out for and how to verify if meta data is available or not ?
It is referring to the existence of files like rust/libcore.rmeta.
i.e. just like C headers, you need to have access to those files for Rust in order to be able to build out-of-tree Rust code.
Those files get produced when you build the main kernel.

I made some progreess. Was able to build and load the kernel with rust enabled. I am able to see the logs in RPi

> dmesg | grep -i rust_minimal
[    0.311983] rust_minimal: Rust minimal sample (init)
[    0.311985] rust_minimal: Am I built-in? true

> dmesg | grep -i rust_print
[    0.311988] rust_print: Rust printing macros sample (init)
[    0.311989] rust_print: Emergency message (level 0) without args
[    0.318058] rust_print: Alert message (level 1) without args
[    0.323742] rust_print: Critical message (level 2) without args
[    0.329690] rust_print: Error message (level 3) without args
[    0.335371] rust_print: Warning message (level 4) without args
[    0.335372] rust_print: Notice message (level 5) without args
[    0.335373] rust_print: Info message (level 6) without args
[    0.335374] rust_print: A line that is continued without args
[    0.335376] rust_print: Emergency message (level 0) with args
[    0.341141] rust_print: Alert message (level 1) with args
[    0.346558] rust_print: Critical message (level 2) with args
[    0.352236] rust_print: Error message (level 3) with args
[    0.357653] rust_print: Warning message (level 4) with args
[    0.357654] rust_print: Notice message (level 5) with args
[    0.357655] rust_print: Info message (level 6) with args
[    0.357656] rust_print: A line that is continued with args
[    0.357658] rust_print: 1
[    0.357660] rust_print: "hello, world"
[    0.357662] rust_print: [samples/rust/rust_print.rs:35:5] c = "hello, world"
[    0.357663] rust_print: "hello, world"

I am now trying to compile the out of tree module and ran into the followinf trouble

  RUSTC [M] /home/.../rust-out-of-tree-module/rust_out_of_tree.o
error: proc macro panicked
  --> /home/.../rust-out-of-tree-module/rust_out_of_tree.rs:7:1
   |
 7 | / module! {
 8 | |     type: RustOutOfTree,
 9 | |     name: "rust_out_of_tree",
10 | |     authors: ["Rust for Linux Contributors"],
11 | |     description: "Rust out-of-tree sample",
12 | |     license: "GPL",
13 | | }
   | |_^
   |
   = help: message: Unknown key "authors". Valid keys are: ["type", "name", "author", "description", "license", "alias", "firmware"].

error[E0425]: cannot find value `__LOG_PREFIX` in the crate root
  --> /home/.../rust-out-of-tree-module/rust_out_of_tree.rs:21:9
   |
21 |         pr_info!("Rust out-of-tree sample (init)\n");
   |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ not found in the crate root
   |
   = note: this error originates in the macro `$crate::print_macro` which comes from the expansion of the macro `pr_info` (in Nightly buil

error[E0425]: cannot find value `__LOG_PREFIX` in the crate root
  --> /home/.../rust-out-of-tree-module/rust_out_of_tree.rs:34:9
   |
34 |         pr_info!("My numbers are {:?}\n", self.numbers);
   |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ not found in the crate root
   |
   = note: this error originates in the macro `$crate::print_macro` which comes from the expansion of the macro `pr_info` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)

error[E0425]: cannot find value `__LOG_PREFIX` in the crate root
  --> /home/../rust-out-of-tree-module/rust_out_of_tree.rs:35:9
   |
35 |         pr_info!("Rust out-of-tree sample (exit)\n");
   |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ not found in the crate root
   |
   = note: this error originates in the macro `$crate::print_macro` which comes from the expansion of the macro `pr_info` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)

error: aborting due to 4 previous errors

For more information about this error, try `rustc --explain E0425`.
make[3]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:294: /home/../rust-out-of-tree-module/rust_out_of_tree.o] Error 1
make[2]: *** [/home/.../Makefile:1945: /home/../rust-out-of-tree-module] Error 2
make[1]: *** [Makefile:224: __sub-make] Error 2
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/../6.12/linux'
make: *** [Makefile:6: default] Error 2

While configuring the kernel, I had

Rust hacking -->

    * Debug assertions [Set to `n`]
    * Allow unoptimized build-time assertions [Set to `n`]

Can this be a possible reason ?
= help: message: Unknown key "authors". Valid keys are: ["type", "name", "author", "description", "license", "alias", "firmware"].
we had a patch that allows authors as a key, but that's not in 6.17, I think
Works ! Thanks !

I have documented my learnings here https://hackmd.io/@akshaim/rust-kernel-rpi5.

Thanks everyone

Commit 38559da6afb2 ("rust: module: introduce authors key"), i.e. v6.16 and later, introduced the authors key.

In the out-of-tree repository, you would need to go back to before commit df508ea15631 ("rust_out_of_tree: use the new authors key").
(The template is only meant to track mainline.)
